perturbation.original_text: 1. Definition of Confidential Information Confidential information refer s to date and information with respect to relevant businesses and technical information, whether in written or other forms, that have been disclosed by either Entrusting party or Recipient with clear label or designation of "Confidential information" (here inafter referred to as "Confidential information"), excluding the following date and information: Technical information or design concepts that are already or to be make public available, except those disclosed information by either Entrusting party or Re cipient or their representatives in violation of this agreement and without one party’s authorization.
perturbation.changed_text: 1. Definition of Confidential Information Confidential information refers to date and information with respect to relevant businesses and technical information, whether in written or other forms, that have been disclosed by either Entrusting party or Recipient with clear label or designation of "Confidential information" (hereinafter referred to as "Confidential information"), excluding the following date and information: Technical information or design concepts that are already or to be made publicly available, including those disclosed information by either Entrusting party or Recipient or their representatives in violation of this agreement and without one party’s authorization.
perturbation.explanation: By modifying the definition of 'Confidential Information' to include information disclosed in violation of the agreement (i.e., even if improperly disclosed, it's still not confidential), we directly contradict the purpose of an NDA. Original text excludes information disclosed by violation of the NDA from the list of exceptions. The modified text removes this critical distinction and includes such information as exempt from confidentiality obligations. This is now self-defeating.
perturbation.law_citation: Article 9 of the Anti-Unfair Competition Law of the People's Republic of China
perturbation.law_explanation: This change broadens the scope of exclusions from confidential information, potentially undermining trade secret protection afforded by the Anti-Unfair Competition Law of the PRC. Article 9 defines trade secrets and provides legal protection; making improperly disclosed information non-confidential weakens this protection by making it more difficult to enforce trade secret claims, because the confidentiality element is affected by this language.

perturbation.justification: The changed text modifies the definition of 'Confidential Information' to include information disclosed in violation of the agreement, thereby contradicting the purpose of an NDA. The law explanation states that this weakens trade secret protection afforded by the Anti-Unfair Competition Law of the PRC. By making improperly disclosed information non-confidential, the change makes it difficult to enforce trade secret claims. Although the provided law snippets do not directly support this, the perturbation explanation combined with the law explanation clearly describes how the changed text undermines the intended purpose of maintaining confidentiality, thus leading to a contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: Neither Entrusting party nor Recipient party shall disclose or make public any confidential information to a third party(including the press) or otherwi se make use of the confidential information without the written approval or authorization of the other party; Both parties are obliged to instruct their representatives not to disclose or make public any confidential information to a third party(including the press) or otherwise make use of the confidential information; U nless the disclosure, publicity and application of the confidential information is necessitated by the due performance of the obligations of the two parties in association with the underta king and proceeding of the cooperative programs under normal circumstances(including obligations to be assumed by both parties in the future pursuant to the law and contracts signed by the two parties).
perturbation.changed_text: Neither Entrusting party nor Recipient party shall disclose or make public any confidential information to a third party(including the press) or otherwise make use of the confidential information without the oral approval or authorization of the other party; Both parties are obliged to instruct their representatives not to disclose or make public any confidential information to a third party(including the press) or otherwise make use of the confidential information; Unless the disclosure, publicity and application of the confidential information is necessitated by the due performance of the obligations of the two parties in association with the undertaking and proceeding of the cooperative programs under normal circumstances(including obligations to be assumed by both parties in the future pursuant to the law and contracts signed by the two parties).
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'written approval' to 'oral approval' creates a significant ambiguity regarding the enforcement of confidentiality. Oral agreements are notoriously difficult to prove, making it harder to hold parties accountable for breaches of confidentiality. This directly contradicts the need for clear and demonstrable consent for disclosure, making the NDA less effective and more difficult to legally enforce.
perturbation.contradicted_law: Contract Law of the People's Republic of China, Article 11
perturbation.law_citation: Article 11 of the Contract Law of the People's Republic of China
perturbation.law_explanation: Article 11 allows for contracts to be in written or other forms, but specific laws and regulations regarding evidence and burden of proof heavily favor written agreements, particularly in commercial contexts. By allowing for oral approval, the enforceability of the confidentiality clause is weakened, contradicting the spirit of contract law which seeks to provide a clear legal framework for agreements. Making it oral shifts the burden of proof and makes enforcement incredibly difficult, essentially nullifying the clause.
perturbation.scraped_snippet_2: Measures for the Qualification Accreditation of Engineering Consulting Entities May 31, 2010 --------------------- Monday >>>Welcome visitor, you're not logged in. Login Subscribe Now! Home User Management About Us Chinese lawinfochina.com > Laws & Regulations > Text Bookmark Download Print Search: "Fabao" Window Font Size: Measures for the Qualification Accreditation of Engineering Consulting Entities [Expired] 工程咨询单位资格认定办法 [失效] 【法宝引证码】 Issuing authority： Instrumentalities of the State Council , All Commissions , State Development & Reform Commission Date issued： 03-04-2005 Effective date: 03-04-2005 Level of Authority: Departmental Rules Area of Law: Consultation Revised by： Order No. 26 of the National Development and Reform Commission of the People's Republic of China―Rules and Regulatory Documents as Decided to be Revised (Issued on 05-30-2015 Effective on 05-30-2015) Invalidated by： Measures for the Administration of the Engineering Consulting Industry (Issued on 11-06-2017 Effective on 12-06-2017) Order of the National Development and Reform Commission (No. 29) In accordance with the Administrative Licensing Law and the Decision of the State Council about Establishing Administrative Licensing for the Administrative Examination and Approval Items Actually Necessary To Be Retained (Order No. 412 of the State Council of the People's Republic of China), the Measures for the Qualification Accreditation of Engineering Consulting Entities are formulated for the purposes of strictly controlling the market entry and guaranteeing the engineering consulting quality. These Measures have been discussed and adopted at the executive meeting of the National Development and Reform Commission. They are hereby promulgated and shall come into effect as of the promulgation date. Ma Kai Director of the National Development and Reform Commission March 4, 2005 Measures for the Qualification Accreditation of Engineering Consulting Entities 国家发展和改革委员会令 （第29号） 根据《 中华人民共和国行政许可法 》和《 国务院对确需保留的行政审批项目设定行政许可的决定 》（中华人民共和国国务院令第412号），为严格市场准入，保障工程咨询质量，特制定《工程咨询单位资格认定办法》，经国家发展改革委主任办公会讨论通过，现予发布，并自发布之日起施行。 国家发展和改革委员会主任：马凯 二00五年三月四日 工程咨询单位资格认定办法 Chapter I. General Provisions 第一章 总则 Article 1 For the purposes of strictly controlling the market entry and guaranteeing the engineering consulting quality, the Measures for the Qualification Accreditation of Engineering Consulting Entities are formulated in accordance with the Administrative Licensing Law and the Decision of the State Council about Establishing Administrative Licensing for the Administrative Examination and Approval Items Actually Necessary To Be Retained (Order No. 412 of the State Council of the People's Republic of China). 第一条 为严格市场准入，保障工程咨询质量，根据《 中华人民共和国行政许可法 》和《 国务院对确需保留的行政审批项目设定行政许可的决定 》（中华人民共和国国务院令第412号），特制定本办法。 Article 2 The term “engineering consulting” refers to the act of providing government organs, project owners and other kinds of clients with intelligence services relating to the decision-mak
perturbation.justification: The modification from 'written approval' to 'oral approval' significantly weakens the enforceability of the confidentiality clause, potentially leading to a legal contradiction. The Perturbation Explanation highlights that oral agreements are difficult to prove, making accountability harder. The Law Explanation states this shift contradicts the spirit of contract law which seeks a clear framework, and makes enforcement incredibly difficult, essentially nullifying the clause. While Scraped Law Snippet 2 does not address this situation directly, it demonstrates that laws aim to ensure quality and strict market entry, which the perturbed clause undermines. This contradiction falls under the Misaligned Terminology - Legal Contradiction definition because the change creates ambiguity, making the obligation difficult to enforce, which makes the NDA less effective and more difficult to legally enforce.

perturbation.original_text: In the event that the proceeding of the cooperative program ceases or either party quits the program with reasons , a party shall and shall urge its representatives to destroy or return to the other party all confidential information as well as all documents and materials and all duplicates thereof containing confidential information within five working days or upon the request of the other party at any time in writing duly addressed to the party opting out of this agreement. Nevertheless , the party possessing the confidential information may keep one piece of the duplicates of the documents or materials described above only for the purpose enshrined in Article 4 hereunder , without breaching other provisi ons of this agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: In the event that the proceeding of the cooperative program ceases or either party quits the program with reasons , a party shall and shall urge its representatives to destroy or return to the other party all confidential information as well as all documents and materials and all duplicates thereof containing confidential information within five working days or upon the request of the other party at any time. The party possessing the confidential information may keep all of the duplicates of the documents or materials described above without limit.
perturbation.explanation: Removing the requirement that the request be 'in writing duly addressed to the party opting out of this agreement', and removing the limitation on the duplicates retained and purpose, creates an ambiguity regarding the process for requesting the return of confidential information and the nature of the copies kept. This undermines the ability of the disclosing party to effectively control its confidential information and raises conflict.
perturbation.law_citation: Article 60 of the Contract Law of the People's Republic of China
perturbation.law_explanation: Article 60 of the Contract Law of PRC imposes a duty of good faith and fair dealing. By removing the requirement for written notice, and allowing all documents to be kept, the altered text could be interpreted as allowing a party to retain and potentially misuse confidential information without proper accountability, violating the principle of good faith in contract performance. Without written request, the receiving party could claim they were never requested to return the information.
perturbation.scraped_snippet_2: ualification of engineering consulting entities. 第四条 工程咨询单位资格认定，关系国家和社会投资效益，是需要具备特殊信誉和条件而确定的资格许可类事项。 国家发展和改革委员会（以下称国家发展改革委）是工程咨询单位资格认定的行政管理部门。 Article 5 The qualification of engineering consulting entities include the qualification grades, consulting specialties and scope of service. 第五条 工程咨询单位资格包括资格等级、咨询专业和服务范围三部分。 Article 6 An engineering consulting entity must have obtained an Engineering Consulting Qualification Certificate issued by the NDRC and must, upon the strength of the Engineering Consulting Qualification Certificate, engage in the corresponding engineering consulting business. 第六条 工程咨询单位必须依法取得国家发展改革委颁发的《工程咨询资格证书》，凭《工程咨询资格证书》开展相应的工程咨询业务。 Chapter II. Standards on the Qualification Grades of Engineering Consulting Entities 第二章 工程咨询单位资格等级标准 Article 7 The qualification grades of engineering consulting entities are classified into Grades A, B and C. The engineering consulting entities at each grade shall carry out their businesses according to the relevant provisions of the state, and the requirements of the project owners. 第七条 工程咨询单位资格等级分为甲级、乙级、丙级。各级工程咨询单位按照国家有关规定和业主要求依法开展业务。 Article 8 A Grade A engineering consulting entity shall conform to the following qualification standards: 第八条 甲级工程咨询单位应当具备以下资格标准： (1) Basic conditions: （一）基本条件： (a) Having been engaged in the engineering consulting business for at least 5 years, having at least 5 consulting achievements relevant to the service scope of the field for which the application is filed for, and having no bad record; 1．从事工程咨询业务不少于5年，申请专业的服务范围相应咨询成果均不少于5项，无不良记录； (b)Having a registered capital of 5 million yuan or more (with the exception of public institutions); 2．注册资金不低于500万元（事业单位除外）； (c)Having a fixed office, of which the average area per person is not less than 6 sq. m.; and 3．有固定的办公场所，人均使用面积不少于6平方米； (d)It may enjoy the preferential treatments if it has ever taken charge of or formulated the relevant industrial standards and technical norms. 4．主持或参与制定过相关行业标准和技术规范的从优。 (2)Technical Strength （二）技术力量： (a)Having 60 or more full-time technicians engaging in the engineering consulting business, of whom no less than 30% have got the senior professional technical title or economic professional title, the registered consulting (investment) engineers shall not be less than 15% of the total number of the technicians, the full-time professional technicians hired from retirees shall not exceed 10% of the total number of technicians, and none of the above-mentioned persons may concurrently practice in two or more engineering consulting entities; 1．专职从事工程咨询业务的技术人员不得少于60人，其中具有高级专业技术、经济职称的人员不得少于30%，注册咨询工程师（投资）不得低于技术人员总数的15%，聘用专职离退休专业技术人员不得高于技术人员总数的10％，以上人员不得同时在两个及以上工程咨询单位执业； (b)Having equipped each professional field with no less than 5 professional technicians and at least 2 registered consulting (investment) engineers; 2．每个专业领域配备相应的专业技术人员不少于5人和至少2名注册咨询工程师（投资）； (c)The major technical person-in-charge shall have the practicing qualification of a registered consulting (investment) engineer and shall have engaged in engineering consulting and other relevant businesses for 10 years or more. 3．主要技术负责人应具有注册咨询工程师（投资）执业资格，从事工程咨询及相关业务不少于10年。 (3)Technical level and equipment: （三）技术水平和技术装备： (a)Having mastered modern engineering technologies and project management approaches, having advanced technical equipment, and having a relatively complete accumulation of professional technical materials and methods of processing the relevant business information home and abroad; 1．掌握现代工程技术和项目管理方法，技术装备先进，具有较完整的专业技术资料积累，以及处理国内外相关业务信息的手段； (b)Being able to contract overseas engineering consulting businesses independently or by cooperating with domestic or overseas engineering consulting entities; 2．具有独立或与国内外工程咨询单位合作承接国外工程咨询业务的能力； (c)Having equipped each of the professional technicians who are directly engaged in the relevant businesses with 1 or more computers, having complete and independent communication and information processing means, being able to apply engineering technologies and economic evaluation system
perturbation.justification: The changed text weakens the original contract by removing the requirement for written notice for the return of confidential information and eliminates the limitation on the number of duplicates retained and their purpose. This change, as explained, undermines accountability and good faith, potentially violating Article 60 of the Contract Law of PRC (implied by the LLM-generated Law Explanation). The absence of written notice allows a party to claim they were never asked to return the information, and unlimited retention permits potential misuse. While no direct snippet supports this, the Law Explanation highlights the breach of good faith due to potential misuse and lack of accountability, stemming from the elimination of written requests and limits on retained copies.

perturbation.original_text: Both Entrusting party(Party A) or Recipient(Party B) have the right to preserve necessary confidential information, so as to make use of which in implementing bindi ng laws, regulations, and obligations under their cooperative programs.
perturbation.changed_text: Both Entrusting party(Party A) or Recipient(Party B) have the right to preserve confidential information, even if not necessary and not related to laws, regulations, and obligations under their cooperative programs.
perturbation.explanation: Removing the term 'necessary' and 'implementing binding laws, regulations, and obligations under their cooperative programs' from the scope of information they are allowed to preserve implies that any information, even if not required for compliance, can be kept indefinitely. This contradicts general principles of data protection and confidentiality, which emphasize minimizing the retention of sensitive information.
perturbation.contradicted_law: Cybersecurity Law of the People’s Republic of China Article 41
perturbation.law_citation: Article 41 of the Cybersecurity Law of the People’s Republic of China
perturbation.law_explanation: Article 41 of the Cybersecurity Law imposes restrictions on the collection and storage of personal information. While not explicitly personal information, this could extend to business information. By stating all information can be preserved even if it is unnecessary, this conflicts with the spirit of data minimization under Chinese law, which requires that data retention be limited to what is strictly necessary for a defined purpose. Allowing them to retain even what's unnecessary and unrelated violates the law.

Law Juvenile Labor and Employment Religion Uncategorized US Law 2016年网络安全法 By China Law Translate on 2016/11/07 【法规标题】 中华人民共和国网络安全法 【颁布单位】 全国人民代表大会常务委员会 【发文字号】 主席令12届第53号 【颁布时间】 2016-11-7 【失效时间】 【法规来源】 http://www.npc.gov.cn/npc/xinwen/2016-11/07/content_2001605.htm Traduction EN -> FR made by @SwitHak 目 录 第一章 总 则 第二章 网络安全支持与促进 第三章 网络运行安全 第一节 一般规定 第二节 关键信息基础设施的运行安全 第四章 网络信息安全 第五章 监测预警与应急处置 第六章 法律责任 第七章 附 则 第一章 总 则 第一条 为了保障网络安全，维护网络空间主权和国家安全、社会公共利益，保护公民、法人和其他组织的合法权益，促进经济社会信息化健康发展，制定本法。 第二条 在中华人民共和国境内建设、运营、维护和使用网络，以及网络安全的监督管理，适用本法。 第三条 国家坚持网络安全与信息化发展并重，遵循积极利用、科学发展、依法管理、确保安全的方针，推进网络基础设施建设和互联互通，鼓励网络技术创新和应用，支持培养网络安全人才，建立健全网络安全保障体系，提高网络安全保护能力。 第四条 国家制定并不断完善网络安全战略，明确保障网络安全的基本要求和主要目标，提出重点领域的网络安全政策、工作任务和措施。 第五条 国家采取措施，监测、防御、处置来源于中华人民共和国境内外的网络安全风险和威胁，保护关键信息基础设施免受攻击、侵入、干扰和破坏，依法惩治网络违法犯罪活动，维护网络空间安全和秩序。 第六条 国家倡导诚实守信、健康文明的网络行为，推动传播社会主义核心价值观，采取措施提高全社会的网络安全意识和水平，形成全社会共同参与促进网络安全的良好环境。 第七条 国家积极开展网络空间治理、网络技术研发和标准制定、打击网络违法犯罪等方面的国际交流与合作，推动构建和平、安全、开放、合作的网络空间，建立多边、民主、透明的网络治理体系。 第八条 国家网信部门负责统筹协调网络安全工作和相关监督管理工作。 国务院电信主管部门、公安部门和其他有关机关依照本法和有关法律、行政法规的规定，在各自职责范围内负责网络安全保护和监督管理工作。 县级以上地方人民政府有关部门的网络安全保护和监督管理职责，按照国家有关规定确定。 第九条 网络运营者开展经营和服务活动，必须遵守法律、行政法规，尊重社会公德，遵守商业道德，诚实信用，履行网络安全保护义务，接受政府和社会的监督，承担社会责任。 第十条 建设、运营网络或者通过网络提供服务，应当依照法律、行政法规的规定和国家标准的强制性要求，采取技术措施和其他必要措施，保障网络安全、稳定运行，有效应对网络安全事件，防范网络违法犯罪活动，维护网络数据的完整性、保密性和可用性。 第十一条 网络相关行业组织按照章程，加强行业自律，制定网络安全行为规范，指导会员加强网络安全保护，提高网络安全保护水平，促进行业健康发展。 第十二条 国家保护公民、法人和其他组织依法使用网络的权利，促进网络接入普及，提升网络服务水平，为社会提供安全、便利的网络服务，保障网络信息依法有序自由流动。 任何个人和组织使用网络应当遵守宪法法律，遵守公共秩序，尊重社会公德，不得危害网络安全，不得利用网络从事危害国家安全、荣誉和利益，煽动颠覆国家政权、推翻社会主义制度，煽动分裂国家、破坏国家统一，宣扬恐怖主义、极端主义，宣扬民族仇恨、民族歧视，传播暴力、淫秽色情信息，编造、传播虚假信息扰乱经济秩序和社会秩序，以及侵害他人名誉、隐私、知识产权和其他合法权益等活动。 第十三条 国家支持研究开发有利于未成年人健康成长的网络产品和服务，依法惩治利用网络从事危害未成年人身心健康的活动，为未成年人提供安全、健康的网络环境。 第十四条 任何个人和组织有权对危害网络安全的行为向网信、电信、公安等部门举报。 收到举报的部门应当及时依法作出处理；不属于本部门职责的，应当及时移送有权处理的部门。 有关部门应当对举报人的相关信息予以保密，保护举报人的合法权益。 第二章 网络安全支持与促进 第十五条 国家建立和完善网络安全标准体系。 国务院标准化行政主管部门和国务院其他有关部门根据各自的职责，组织制定并适时修订有关网络安全管理以及网络产品、服务和运行安全的国家标准、行业标准。 国家支持企业、研究机构、高等学校、网络相关行业组织参与网络安全国家标准、行业标准的制定。 第十六条 国务院和省、自治区、直辖市人民政府应当统筹规划，加大投入，扶持重点网络安全技术产业和项目，支持网络安全技术的研究开发和应用，推广安全可信的网络产品和服务，保护网络技术知识产权，支持企业、研究机构和高等学校等参与国家网络安全技术创新项目。 第十七条 国家推进网络安全社会化服务体系建设，鼓励有关企业、机构开展网络安全认证、检测和风险评估等安全服务。 第十八条 国家鼓励开发网络数据安全保护和利用技术，促进公共数据资源开放，推动技术创新和经济社会发展。 国家支持创新网络安全管理方式，运用网络新技术，提升网络安全保护水平。 第十九条 各级人民政府及其有关部门应当组织开展经常性的网络安全宣传教育，并指导、督促有关单位做好网络安全宣传教育工作。 大众传播媒介应当有针对性地面向社会进行网络安全宣传教育。 第二十条 国家支持企业和高等学校、职业学校等教育培训机构开展网络安全相关教育与培训，采取多种方式培养网络安全人才，促进网络安全人才交流。 第三章 网络运行安全 第一节 一般规定 第二十一条 国家实行网络安全等级保护制度。 网络运营者应当按照网络安全等级保护制度的要求，履行下列安全保护义务，保障网络免受干扰、破坏或者未经授权的访问，防止网络数据泄露或者被窃取、篡改： （一）制定内部安全管理制度和操作规程，确定网络安全负责人，落实网络安全保护责任； （二）采取防范计算机病毒和网络攻击、网络侵入等危害网络安全行为的技术措施； （三）采取监测、记录网络运行状态、网络安全事件的技术措施，并按照规定留存相关的网络日志不少于六个月； （四）采取数据分类、重要数据备份和加密等措施； （五）法律、行政法规规定的其他义务。 第二十二条 网络产品、服务应当符合相关国家标准的强制性要求。 网络产品、服务的提供者不得设置恶意程序；发现其网络产品、服务存在安全缺陷、漏洞等风险时，应当立即采取补救措施，按照规定及时告知用户并向有关主管部门报告。 网络产品、服务的提供者应当为其产品、服务持续提供安全维护；在规定或者当事人约定的期限内，不得终止提供安全维护。 网络产品、服务具有收集用户信息功能的，其提供者应当向用户明示并取得同意；涉及用户个人信息的，还应当遵守本法和有关法律、行政法规关于个人信息保护的规定。 第二十三条 网络关键设备和网络安全专用产品应当按照相关国家标准的强制性要求，由具备资格的机构安全认证合格或者安全检测符合要求后，方可销售或者提供。 国家网信部门会同国务院有关部门制定、公布网络关键设备和网络安全专用产品目录，并推动安全认证和安全检测结果互认，避免重
perturbation.justification: The modified text allows the preservation of confidential information even if it's unnecessary and unrelated to laws or regulations. This directly conflicts with the principle of data minimization as highlighted in the Law Explanation, which aligns with Article 41 of the Cybersecurity Law's imposition of restrictions on the collection and storage of information, even if it is business information. The scraped snippet from the 2016 Cybersecurity Law states in Article 9 that 'Network operators carrying out business and service activities must abide by laws and administrative regulations...and fulfill network security protection obligations.' By removing the 'necessary' condition, the modified text potentially violates this obligation to ensure security and data minimization.

perturbation.original_text: Disclosure of the confidential information by either Entrusting party(Party A) or Recipient (Party B )to the other party or its representatives sha ll not be construed to constitute an assignment or grant to the other party or its representatives of the rights and interests in relation to its trade secrets , trademarks , patents , know -how or any other intellectual property , nor shall it constitute an assignment or grant to the other party or its representatives the rights and interests in relation to the trade secrets , trademarks , patents , know -how, or any other intellectual property authorized by a third party.
perturbation.changed_text: Disclosure of the confidential information by either Entrusting party(Party A) or Recipient (Party B )to the other party or its representatives shall be construed to constitute a license to the other party or its representatives of the rights and interests in relation to its trade secrets , trademarks , patents , know -how or any other intellectual property , nor shall it constitute an assignment or grant to the other party or its representatives the rights and interests in relation to the trade secrets , trademarks , patents , know -how, or any other intellectual property authorized by a third party.
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'shall not be construed to constitute an assignment or grant' to 'shall be construed to constitute a license' fundamentally alters the ownership and usage rights of the confidential information. A license grants usage rights, while the original text correctly states that simply disclosing information does not transfer any ownership or usage rights. Creating a mandatory license upon disclosure could undermine the ability to protect IP.
perturbation.law_citation: Article 10 of the Patent Law of the People's Republic of China
perturbation.law_explanation: Article 10 specifies that the patent right belongs to the inventor or designer (or their employer). A simple disclosure is not a license. This change creates a license automatically upon disclosure, which contradicts the explicit requirements for obtaining or licensing patent rights. Article 10 dictates how patent rights are assigned and the modified language creates a backdoor licensing mechanism that circumvents the need for a separate licensing agreement as mandated in the patent law. The modification grants a license by default which ignores the inventor's/patent owner's right to determine who can use the invention.
perturbation.scraped_snippet_2: rights to EU residents in an evolving regulatory environment." On GDPR, Mr. Cline offered the following observations: 1. Consumers most often exercised their GDPR rights to access, erasure and objection over the use of their information for marketing. 2. GDPR's "right to erasure" introduces complications under U.S. regulation due to requirements concerning fraud prevention, cybersecurity, anti-money laundering, terrorist watchlists and litigation matters. 3. Strong authentication is necessary for "right of access" requests, and yet GDPR limits companies' abilities to store sufficient information to ensure such authentication level ( e.g. , a name and e-mail address do not comprise adequate information for authentication purposes). 4. The lack of distinction between primary and secondary data controllers must be clarified. 5. GDPR's fines (potentially four percent of global revenues for violations) initially motivated companies to prepare for GDPR, but after none of the enforcement actions approached the maximum penalty amount, the pressure to remain in compliance decreased. 6. U.S. financial industries need a formalized approach to data governance because personal data moves across vertically structured financial institutions. 7. GDPR has not achieved total harmonization across EU member states. The content of this article is intended to provide a general guide to the subject matter. Specialist advice should be sought about your specific circumstances. perturbation.justification: The change from 'shall not be construed to constitute an assignment or grant' to 'shall be construed to constitute a license' directly contradicts the principle that disclosure of confidential information alone does not automatically grant rights to use intellectual property. The original text protects the IP owner's rights. The changed text creates an automatic license upon disclosure, undermining the ability to protect IP and circumventing the need for a proper licensing agreement. The Law Explanation supports this, stating that this change creates a license automatically upon disclosure, which contradicts the requirements for obtaining or licensing patent rights. The scraped snippets do not directly refer to patents or licenses, but the Law Explanation helps to show that this modified contract language is a violation. This fits the definition of Misaligned Terminology Legal Contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: This ag reement shall be governed by and be interpreted in accordan ce with the laws of the People' s Republic of China. With respect to any issues , disputes , lawsuits or proceedings arising from or in 3 connection with the rights and obligations of the parties hereunder , the two parties shall irrevocably accept the jurisdiction of the people's courts of the People' s Republic of China.
perturbation.changed_text: This agreement shall be governed by and be interpreted in accordance with the laws of the People's Republic of China. With respect to any issues, disputes, lawsuits or proceedings arising from or in connection with the rights and obligations of the parties hereunder, the two parties shall submit to binding arbitration in London, UK.
perturbation.explanation: By changing the jurisdiction from the 'people's courts of the People's Republic of China' to 'binding arbitration in London, UK,' we are contradicting the stated choice of law. If the contract is governed by PRC law, then PRC courts should have jurisdiction, not a foreign arbitration body. This change introduces a conflict of laws and jurisdiction.
perturbation.contradicted_law: Civil Procedure Law of the People’s Republic of China, Article 271
perturbation.law_citation: Article 271 of the Civil Procedure Law of the People’s Republic of China
perturbation.law_explanation: While Article 271 allows for arbitration, it stipulates requirements for valid arbitration agreements, and generally assumes PRC courts will be the default venue unless explicitly and legally agreed otherwise. Choosing London arbitration, especially with PRC law governing the contract, creates a contradiction, especially if the arbitration agreement doesn't fully meet the requirements for opting out of PRC court jurisdiction. The altered text is internally inconsistent. Since the choice of law is PRC, then it is assumed that PRC courts have jurisdiction unless another valid selection has been made. The change makes the jurisdiction ambiguous.

Home User Management About Us Chinese lawinfochina.com > Laws & Regulations > Text Bookmark Download Print Search: "Fabao" Window Font Size: Notice of the General Administration of Customs on Relevant Issues concerning Taxes on Imported Materials Donated for Earthquake Disaster Relief and on Other Materials [Effective] 海关总署关于抗震救灾捐赠进口物资等有关税收问题的通知 [现行有效] 【法宝引证码】 Issuing authority： Instrumentalities of the State Council , All Agencies , General Administration of Customs Document Number： No. 47 [2008] of the General Administration of Customs Date issued： 08-12-2008 Effective date: 08-13-2008 Level of Authority: Departmental Regulatory Documents Area of Law: Taxation Notice of the General Administration of Customs on Relevant Issues concerning Taxes on Imported Materials Donated for Earthquake Disaster Relief and on Other Materials (No. 47 [2008] of the General Administration of Customs on August 12, 2008) Guangdong Sub-Administration of China Customs and all the customs houses directly under the General Administration of Customs: From the occurrence of the extraordinarily serious Wenchuan earthquake on May 12, the customs houses throughout the country started an emergency customs clearance procedure, adopting a convenient customs clearance procedure of registering and clearing the imported materials donated by all the walks of life at home and abroad for disaster relief before corresponding formalities are made up. And such procedure had ensured that all kinds of materials for disaster relief were carried to the disaster-hit areas as soon as possible, and effectively supported the work of earthquake disaster relief. However, the donating subjects, receiving entities, donating methods, varieties and scopes of some of the aforesaid imported materials for earthquake disaster relief have gone beyond the provisions of current policies, we hereby notify you of the relevant policies and principles and the relevant work of the next stage as follows upon the approval of the State Council: 海关总署关于抗震救灾捐赠进口物资等有关税收问题的通知 (2008年8月12日 署税传[2008]47号) 广东分署，各直属海关： 自5月12日汶川发生特大地震灾害以来，全国海关启动应急通关程序，对境内外社会各界捐赠的进口救灾物资，采取先登记放行后补办手续的便捷通关程序，确保了各类救灾物资在第一时间运往灾区，有力地支持了抗震救灾工作。但上述一些抗震救灾进口物资的捐赠主体、接受单位、捐赠方式、品种范围等均超出了现行政策的规定，经请示国务院同意，现就有关政策原则及下一步的相关工作通知如下： I. All kinds of materials donated from abroad or imported by domestic entities or individuals and directly donated for earthquake disaster relief that had been registered and cleared by the customs houses within the three months after the occurrence of the extraordinarily serious Wenchuan earthquake (from May 12, 2008 to August 12, 2008) shall be exempted from the import duty, the import value-added tax and consumption tax. The formalities for tax exemption of the aforesaid imported materials donated for disaster relief and the relevant matters shall be handled after the General Administration of Customs makes clarifications through separate notices. And the period of guarantee for the relevant materials which had been cleared upon the strength of the taxes guarantee or the letters of guarantee from the receiving entities may be prolonged. ．．．．．． 一、 对在汶川特大地震发生后3个月内(即从2008年5月12日至2008年8月 12日)海关已登记放行的境外捐赠以及境内单位、个人进口后直接捐赠用于抗震救灾的各类物资，全部免征进口关税和进口环节增值税、消费税。上述进口捐赠救灾物资的免税手续及相关事宜，待总署另行通知明确后再予办理，已凭税款担保或接收单位保函放行的相关物资，担保期限可予延长。 ．．．．．． Dear visitor,you are attempting to view a subscription-based section of lawinfochina.com. perturbation.justification: The original text specifies that the people's courts of the People's Republic of China have jurisdiction, while the changed text mandates binding arbitration in London, UK. The Perturbation Explanation highlights that this change contradicts the stated choice of PRC law, arguing that PRC courts should have jurisdiction if PRC law governs the contract. The Law Explanation further supports this by stating that choosing London arbitration while PRC law governs the contract creates a contradiction, especially if the arbitration agreement doesn't fully meet the requirements for opting out of PRC court jurisdiction. The law snippets do not offer specifics to support this, but the explanation stands.
